subject: Faded crosswalk at St. Charles and 25th Ave — dangerous for kids
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I walk my grandchildren to school every morning and the crosswalk at St. Charles and 25th is nearly invisible. The white lines are worn down to maybe 20% of what they should be. Drivers aren't stopping. It's been this way since at least last fall and with spring break over, foot traffic is back up. Can the village get this repainted before someone gets hurt?

subject: Faded crosswalk at St. Charles and 25th Ave — dangerous for kids
body_clean: Ms. Kozlowski, I went out this morning to look. You're right — that crosswalk is overdue. We have a thermoplastic striping crew on St. Charles the week of April 28th and I've added the 25th Ave intersection as a priority stop. Should be done by end of that week, weather permitting. Appreciate you flagging it.

subject: Vacant storefront at 588 St. Charles — is the village tracking it?
body_clean: Hello Mayor, the building at 588 St. Charles has been empty going on three years now. Plywood on one window, weeds through the sidewalk crack out front, and last week I noticed what looked like a broken utility pipe near the rear wall. Is the village working with the owner on a plan? I'd hate to see it become a full blight situation. I've lived on Bellwood Ave my whole life and that corridor matters to me.

subject: Vacant storefront at 588 St. Charles — is the village tracking it?
body_clean: Mr. Tillman, the property at 588 St. Charles is on our vacant-building registry under Ordinance 14-2019. The owner was issued a violation notice in February for the unsecured window and overgrown sidewalk — compliance was partial. I'll schedule a follow-up inspection within ten days. If the pipe issue is in the public right-of-way I'll loop in Public Works. There's no confirmed redevelopment agreement yet, but economic development staff are aware of the parcel.

subject: Facade Improvement Grant — is El Faro eligible?
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I heard at the chamber breakfast last week that the village has a facade grant program for St. Charles Rd businesses. We've been wanting to replace our awning and repaint the front since 2022 but cash flow has been tight. El Faro has been at 612 St. Charles for eleven years — does that longevity count in the scoring? And is there a deadline coming up for the next application window? I'd rather not miss it again.

subject: Facade Improvement Grant — is El Faro eligible?
body_clean: The Mayor asked me to follow up. El Faro would absolutely be eligible — the program covers commercial properties on St. Charles between Mannheim and 50th Ave, and years in operation is a scoring factor. Eleven years is strong. The next application window opens November 4th with a December 6th deadline. I'm attaching the guidelines and scoring rubric. Feel free to call the office at 708-555-0142 if you have questions while filling it out.

subject: Parking blocking the PACE bus stop near 618 St. Charles
body_clean: Mayor — every weekday morning there are two or three cars parked right at the PACE bus stop in front of 618 St. Charles. People getting off the bus have to step into the travel lane to get around them. I've called 311 twice. The first time the officer came out but the cars were already gone. This is a regular problem, not a one-off. Can the village put up a proper no-parking-bus-stop sign and add some morning enforcement to that block?

subject: Parking blocking the PACE bus stop near 618 St. Charles
body_clean: Ms. Petersen, we're adding a 7:00–8:30 a.m. patrol check of that block to the Tuesday/Thursday rotation starting next week. I also confirmed with Public Works that the bus stop zone isn't marked with a post sign — just faded pavement stenciling. A work order is in for a proper R7-107 bus stop sign. I'll follow up once the sign is installed. If you see it happening again in the meantime, please call dispatch at 708-555-0110 with the plate number if you can get it.

subject: Zoning question — small grocery at 560 St. Charles
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, my family is looking at the retail space at 560 St. Charles to open a small Mexican grocery — fresh produce, dry goods, and a small deli counter. Before I sign anything with the landlord I need to confirm the zoning allows it and understand what permits are required. I also heard IDOT is doing work on St. Charles this year — will that affect deliveries? I can meet in person if that's easier than email.

subject: Zoning question — small grocery at 560 St. Charles
body_clean: Mr. Guerrero, thank you for reaching out — a grocery at 560 St. Charles is exactly the kind of neighborhood business we want to support on that corridor. I'm having my assistant set up a time for you to sit down with our community development staff, who can cover the B-2 zoning classification, the change-of-use permit, and the health department checklist in one meeting. I've also cc'd our Public Works director on the IDOT delivery question — Frank can tell you which side streets are available for staging. Looking forward to meeting you.

subject: Double-parking on St. Charles between 25th and 26th — every Saturday
body_clean: Hello Mayor, I run errands on St. Charles every Saturday and between roughly 9 and 11 a.m. the block between 25th and 26th Ave is basically one lane because delivery trucks and personal vehicles are doubled up on both sides. I almost clipped someone opening their door last week. I understand deliveries have to happen but is there a designated loading zone or a time window that could help? It's been this way every weekend all spring.

subject: Development interest — corner parcel at St. Charles and 50th Ave
body_clean: Mayor, I've been watching the corner parcel at St. Charles and 50th Ave for about a year. I develop small mixed-use projects and I think there's a real case for a two-story build with retail ground floor and four or five units above. Who do I contact for a preliminary zoning conversation? I'm not ready for a formal application — I just want to understand if the village would be receptive before I commission a survey. Happy to meet whenever is convenient.

subject: Development interest — corner parcel at St. Charles and 50th Ave
body_clean: Mr. Rousseau, the Mayor asked me to connect you with our Community Development Administrator — she handles exactly these pre-application conversations, no formal submission required. You can talk through density, setbacks, and the village's appetite for mixed-use on that stretch without committing to anything. I'll send a calendar invite for a call later this week. The village has been actively trying to attract infill development on St. Charles, so I think you'll find the conversation encouraging.

subject: Broken sidewalk slab in front of 604 St. Charles — trip hazard
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, the sidewalk slab in front of 604 St. Charles is cracked and lifted about two inches on one edge. I tripped on it last Tuesday evening carrying grocery bags. It was dark and I didn't see the raised edge — my ankle is still a bit sore but I'm okay. I know winter isn't ideal for concrete work, but could the village put up a hazard cone or do a temporary patch? I walk that block every day.

subject: Facade grant application for 571 St. Charles — incomplete notice question
body_clean: Hi, I submitted a facade grant application for my alterations shop at 571 St. Charles last month and just got a letter saying it's incomplete — but there's no explanation of what's missing. My photos were phone photos; is that the problem? I put a lot of work into that application and I'd hate to lose my spot over a technicality. Can someone review it with me before the deadline?

subject: Facade grant application for 571 St. Charles — incomplete notice question
body_clean: Ms. Subramaniam, I pulled your file. The incomplete notice was triggered by a missing signed contractor estimate — not the photos. Phone photos are perfectly acceptable for the before documentation. You have until May 30th to submit the estimate. Email it to my attention and I'll make sure it's attached to your application before the deadline. You're in good shape otherwise — six years at that location is solid on the scoring rubric.

subject: Opening a barbershop at 548 St. Charles — zoning and incentives
body_clean: Mayor, I've been cutting hair for fifteen years and I'm finally ready to open my own shop. I'm looking at 548 St. Charles — it was a nail salon before. My real estate agent says a barbershop might not need a full change-of-use permit since both are personal service uses, but I want the village to confirm that before I put down a deposit. I'd also love to know if there are any small-business incentives available on St. Charles. Who should I talk to?

subject: Outdoor dining permit — been waiting eleven weeks
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, we applied in March to use two parking spaces out front of El Faro for a seasonal outdoor patio enclosure. We have health department sign-off and the insurance rider is ready. The permit application has been sitting with the village for eleven weeks with no decision. Taste of Bellwood is right around the corner and we were counting on that patio for the summer. Is there anything you can do to move it along?
